title: Grabowski operator surface
goal: Reduce Grabowski operator decision entropy by adding a read-only situation spine, a small intent-shaped grip surface, friction feedback, evidence-bound recall, durable jobs and enforceable session attenuation.
completion: A fresh operator can read one situation output and know current state, blockers, stale-tool uncertainty and the next allowed grip.
Grip list/run exposes small receipt-bound intents instead of the full tool drawer.
Repeated friction produces proposal-only next-grip or next-task recommendations.
Operator recall is evidence-bound and cannot replace receipts, reviews or Bureau completion.
Durable job and session capability slices are registered but remain gated behind reliable read-side evidence.

metadata.source_summary: User asked to turn the Grabowski operator-surface ideas into a Bureau plan after review of situation, grips, frozen snapshots, friction loop, push jobs, episodic memory and session attenuation.

relationship_to_existing.GRIP-ROADMAP-V1: complements and narrows operator-facing work; does not replace queued grip roadmap tasks
relationship_to_existing.docs/grabowski-restplan-v2.md: reuses capability-profile direction and converts it into task-shaped follow-up

metadata.priority_decision: situation spine before recall; grip surface before friction automation; push and capability enforcement later
